name: Coincheck Cryptocurrency Exchange API
what_it_does: Japanese cryptocurrency exchange REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and developers to access Bitcoin and altcoin trading with JPY (Japanese Yen) pairs, manage orders, access account information, and stream real-time data, regulated by Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA) and operating under Monex Group ownership after the 2018 $530M NEM hack.
use_cases: Trading Bitcoin with JPY via Coincheck REST API for Japanese market trading strategies
Accessing Japanese yen crypto pairs via Coincheck API for JPY-denominated portfolio management
Integrating Coincheck data via API for Japanese crypto fintech applications
Streaming real-time BTC/JPY order book via Coincheck WebSocket for market analysis
Managing crypto assets via Coincheck API for Japanese compliance-aware applications
not_for: Non-Japanese traders without JPY exposure (limited pairs outside JPY)
Wide altcoin selection (Coincheck supports limited coins: BTC, ETH, ETC, LSK, XRP, XEM, LTC, BCH, MONA, IOST, ADA, XLM, etc.)
Institutional-grade services without Japanese regulatory requirements
best_when: A Japanese developer or trader needs a regulated JPY-cryptocurrency exchange API that complies with Japan's FSA regulations and is backed by established financial group (Monex).
avoid_when: You're outside Japan, need wide altcoin selection, or require derivatives and margin trading.

auth.notes: API Key + API Secret for HMAC-SHA256 signing. ACCESS-KEY header (API key). ACCESS-SIGNATURE header (HMAC-SHA256 of nonce + URL + body). ACCESS-NONCE header (Unix millisecond timestamp — must increase per request). No OAuth2. No webhooks. Simple authentication pattern. Private endpoints require all three headers.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; maker 0% / taker 0% (as of 2024 — verify current)
pricing.paid_tiers: No subscription tiers; spread-based revenue model
pricing.notes: Japanese company. Founded 2012 (Koichiro Wada and Yusuke Otsuka). Largest Japanese crypto exchange by volume historically. January 2018 hack: $530M in NEM (XEM) stolen — largest crypto theft at the time. Acquired by Monex Group (major Japanese brokerage) 2018 for ¥3.6B. Post-hack rebuilding. FSA registered virtual currency exchange (JVCEA member). JPY pairs primary. IEO (Initial Exchange Offering) platform. Coincheck NFT marketplace. Japanese language primary. ISMS (ISO 27001) certified. 1.7M+ users.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HMAC-SHA256 with nonce. ACCESS headers. Japanese FSA regulated. Monex Group owned. 2018 $530M hack history. JPY-focused. Japanese documentation primary. ISO 27001 certified.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order reads idempotent by order ID. Nonce must increment — prevents replay. Cancel idempotent by order ID. Order creation not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: ACCESS-NONCE must be monotonically increasing — Unix millisecond timestamp works but cannot reuse
Japanese documentation is primary — English docs may lag behind or be incomplete
JPY pairs are primary — limited USD/USDT equivalent pairs available
2018 $530M NEM hack — company rebuilt security under Monex Group ownership
Rate limits undocumented — implement conservative rate limiting to avoid blocks

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 with monotonic nonce (replay protection). Japanese FSA regulation (strict). ISO 27001 certified post-hack. Monex Group financial oversight. 2018 $530M hack — rebuilding security. JPY transactions — Japanese financial regulations apply. No OAuth2. Nonce prevents replay attacks.
